protocols.name: Colorectal
protocols.description: Vanderbilt University — Anesthesia Practice Standardization Protocol v12.2017

Medications.name: Gabapentin
Medications.summary: 100–300 mg PO 1 hr before OR; reduce for age >65, OSA
Medications.content:
Gabapentin

Orders by Perioperative Consult Service

  • 100–300 mg PO 1 hour before OR time
  • Reduce to 100 mg if >65y
  • Consider omitting or 100 mg if >75y
  • Dose reduction for OSA patients

Medications.name: Acetaminophen (Tylenol)
Medications.summary: 1000 mg PO 1 hr before OR; reduce to 650 mg if <70 kg
Medications.content:
Acetaminophen (Tylenol)

Orders by Perioperative Consult Service

  • 1000 mg PO 1 hour before OR time
  • Reduce to 650 mg PO if <70 kg
  • Do not use if history of liver disease

Medications.name: PONV Prophylaxis — Preoperative
Medications.summary: TIVA preferred; scopolamine patch only if >3 risk factors and hx of PONV
Medications.content:
PONV Prophylaxis — Preoperative

*TIVA preferred for all colorectal patients.

  • Scopolamine patch if >3 Apfel risk factors — only if hx of PONV or motion sickness
  • Do NOT use if >65y with OSA, dementia, or anticholinergic use

See PONV Guidelines on CEBA Website.

Blocks.name: TAP Block — Mini-Lap / Hand-Assisted
Blocks.summary: Bilateral TAP ± rectus sheath; ropivacaine 0.25% + dexamethasone
Blocks.content:
Regional Anesthesia — Mini-Lap or Hand-Assisted Laparoscopy

Pfannenstiel Incision

  • Bilateral TAP Blocks
  • Ropivacaine 0.25% + Dexamethasone 4 mg — 25–30 mL/side

Periumbilical Incision

  • Bilateral TAP + Bilateral Rectus Sheath blocks
  • Ropivacaine 0.25% + Dexamethasone 2 mg — 10–15 mL/side

Blocks.name: TAP Block — Ostomy Takedown / Creation
Blocks.summary: Unilateral TAP block; ropivacaine 0.25% + dexamethasone 4 mg, 25–30 mL
Blocks.content:
Regional Anesthesia — Ostomy Takedown / Creation

  • Unilateral TAP Block
  • Ropivacaine 0.25% + Dexamethasone 4 mg — 25–30 mL

Blocks.name: TAP Block — Laparotomy
Blocks.summary: Bilateral TAP + rectus sheath; thoracic epidural if incision above T8
Blocks.content:
Regional Anesthesia — Laparotomy

  • Bilateral TAP + Bilateral Rectus Sheath blocks
  • Ropivacaine 0.25% + Dexamethasone 2 mg — 10–15 mL/side
Thoracic Epidural: Place if incision extends above T8. Discuss with surgeon before planning.

Anesthesia.name: TIVA with Propofol
Anesthesia.summary: ***TIVA preferred*** — avoid volatile unless propofol allergy
Anesthesia.content:
TIVA with Propofol

Use propofol infusion as the primary anesthetic agent. Please avoid volatile agents unless the patient has a propofol allergy.

Anesthesia.name: Opioid Minimization
Anesthesia.summary: No induction opioids; ketamine + esmolol for HR control
Anesthesia.content:
Opioid Minimization

  • No induction opioids
  • Propofol or volatile anesthetic + ketamine
  • Esmolol for heart rate control

Anesthesia.name: Ketamine
Anesthesia.summary: 0.5 mg/kg bolus + 5 mcg/kg/min until fascia closure
Anesthesia.content:
Ketamine — Intraoperative

  • 0.5 mg/kg IV bolus with induction
  • 5 mcg/kg/min after induction until fascia closure
  • Reduce bolus to 0.25 mg/kg in patients >65y

Anesthesia.name: Lidocaine Infusion
Anesthesia.summary: 1.5 mg/kg bolus then 2 mg/min drip to case end; restart in PACU
Anesthesia.content:
Lidocaine Infusion — Intraoperative

  • 1.5 mg/kg bolus at induction, then 2 mg/min drip to case end
  • Continues in PACU per postop order set

Contraindications

  • Unstable heart disease, Recent MI, Heart block, Heart Failure
  • Electrolyte disturbances, Liver disease, Seizure disorder
  • Current anti-arrhythmic therapy (amiodarone, sotalol)

Anesthesia.name: Ketorolac
Anesthesia.summary: 30 mg IV at fascia closure; reduce to 15 mg if >65y, CrCl<30, or <50 kg
Anesthesia.content:
Ketorolac — Intraoperative

  • 30 mg IV at fascia closure
  • Reduce to 15 mg IV if >65y, CrCl <30, or weight <50 kg
  • Consider avoiding with history of renal dysfunction or GI bleed

Anesthesia.name: Methadone
Anesthesia.summary: Assess at emergence; 5 mg IV boluses q5–10 min if opioids needed
Anesthesia.content:
Methadone

Assess at emergence for need. Majority of patients do not require intraoperative opioids.

  • If needed: 5 mg IV boluses q5–10 min on emergence or in PACU

PONV Prophylaxis.name: PONV Prophylaxis — Intraoperative
PONV Prophylaxis.summary: Dexamethasone 8 mg post-induction; ondansetron 4 mg 30 min pre-emergence
PONV Prophylaxis.content:
PONV Prophylaxis — Intraoperative

  • Dexamethasone: 8 mg IV after induction (unless given in TAP blocks)
  • Ondansetron: 4 mg IV 30 min prior to emergence

Fluid & Hemodynamic Management.name: Intraoperative Fluid Strategy
Fluid & Hemodynamic Management.summary: LR/Plasma-Lyte only; SV-guided via Clearsight; 3 mL/kg/hr maintenance
Fluid & Hemodynamic Management.content:
Intraoperative Fluid Strategy

  • LR or Plasma-Lyte only — NO NORMAL SALINE
  • Do not replace deficit empirically
  • 250 mL bolus after induction; repeat if delta SV >+10%
  • Maintenance: 3 mL/kg/hr
  • MAP <75 or >10% below baseline + euvolemia → phenylephrine up to 0.5 mcg/kg/min
  • Target UOP ≥0.5 mL/kg/hr

NO BUGS (SSI Reduction).name: Normothermia
NO BUGS (SSI Reduction).summary: Goal core temp >36.0°C; forced air warming from holding room
NO BUGS (SSI Reduction).content:
Normothermia

Goal: Core temp >36.0°C

  • Forced air warming beginning in the holding room
  • Warm all IV fluids; warm room if T <36.0°C

NO BUGS (SSI Reduction).name: Oxygenation / Tidal Volume
NO BUGS (SSI Reduction).summary: FiO₂ ≥0.8, TV 8 mL/kg IBW, PEEP ≥6 cmH₂O throughout
NO BUGS (SSI Reduction).content:
Oxygenation / Tidal Volume

  • FiO₂ ≥ 0.8 with TV 8 mL/kg IBW + PEEP ≥6 cmH₂O
  • Nasal cannula in PACU to maintain SaO₂ 100%
  • NC @ 2 L/min prior to PACU discharge and 24 hrs postop

NO BUGS (SSI Reduction).name: Glucose Control
NO BUGS (SSI Reduction).summary: Goal 140–180 mg/dL; FSBG preop on all colorectal patients
NO BUGS (SSI Reduction).content:
Glucose Control

Goal: 140–180 mg/dL. Do NOT give dextrose unless glucose <60.

  • Check FSBG preop on all colorectal patients
  • Diabetic: check q1hr intraop
  • Non-diabetic ≥120 preop: recheck after 1 hr; if still ≥120, q1hr intraop

NO BUGS (SSI Reduction).name: Antibiotics
NO BUGS (SSI Reduction).summary: Proper drug/dose/timing (<1 hr of incision); re-dose per protocol
NO BUGS (SSI Reduction).content:
Antibiotics

  • Proper drug, dose, timing (<1 hr of incision), and re-dosing strategy
  • See antibiotic dosing chart in VPIMS. OR Pharmacy: 2-4897

Pain Management.name: Thoracic Epidural
Pain Management.summary: Bupivacaine 0.1% + hydromorphone 10 mcg/mL; remove at bowel function return
Pain Management.content:
Thoracic Epidural — Postoperative

  • Bupivacaine 0.1% + hydromorphone 10 mcg/mL
  • Remove day prior to anticipated discharge
  • Remove once patient has flatus or return of bowel function

Pain Management.name: Ketamine Infusion
Pain Management.summary: 2.5 mcg/kg/min × 48 hrs; requires 2nd IV + NS carrier at 10 cc/hr
Pain Management.content:
Ketamine Infusion — Postoperative

  • 2.5 mcg/kg/min × 48 hours (high concentration bag — not continued from OR)
  • Requires 2nd IV + NS carrier at 10 cc/hr
  • Max weight: 100 kg

Contraindications

  • Increased ICP, history of PTSD or psychotic states, increased IOP

Pain Management.name: Lidocaine Infusion
Pain Management.summary: Continue 24 hrs postop; weight-based: <70 kg→1 mg/min, 70–100→1.5, >100→2
Pain Management.content:
Lidocaine Infusion — Postoperative

Continue for 24 hours from PACU or after TEC removed.

WeightDose
<70 kg1 mg/min IV
70–100 kg1.5 mg/min IV
>100 kg2 mg/min IV

Pain Management.name: Gabapentin
Pain Management.summary: 300–600 mg PO q8h POD0 to discharge; continue ×14 days at home
Pain Management.content:
Gabapentin — Postoperative

  • 300–600 mg PO q8h starting POD0 until discharge
  • Lower dose for >65y or significant sedation/dizziness
  • Discharge: continue ×14 days; taper if on >300 mg q8h

Pain Management.name: Acetaminophen
Pain Management.summary: 1000 mg PO q8h POD0 to discharge (max 4000 mg/day)
Pain Management.content:
Acetaminophen — Postoperative

  • 1000 mg PO q8h POD0 to discharge (max 4000 mg/24 hrs)
  • Reduce to 650 mg q6h if <70 kg

Pain Management.name: Ketorolac
Pain Management.summary: 30 mg IV q6h ×3 days; reduce to 15 mg if >65y, CrCl<30, or <50 kg
Pain Management.content:
Ketorolac — Postoperative

  • 30 mg IV q6h × 3 days
  • Reduce to 15 mg if >65y, CrCl <30, or weight <50 kg

Pain Management.name: Opioid PRN
Pain Management.summary: Tramadol 50 mg q4h (no SSRI/SNRI); oxycodone 5 mg; hydromorphone IV breakthrough
Pain Management.content:
Opioid PRN

Use lowest possible dose and frequency.

  • Tramadol 50 mg q4h PRN — do not use with SSRI/SNRI
  • Oxycodone 5 mg PO q4h PRN
  • Hydromorphone 0.25 mg IV q4h PRN (opioid naïve or >65y); 0.5 mg if tolerant

Discharge

  • PRN q6h × 3–5 days (12 pills total); tramadol preferred (Schedule IV)

PONV Treatment.name: Ondansetron
PONV Treatment.summary: 4 mg IV/PO q6h PRN — first line

PONV Treatment.name: Promethazine
PONV Treatment.summary: 6.25–12.5 mg IV/PO PRN — ensure patent IV before injecting
PONV Treatment.content:
Promethazine

  • 6.25–12.5 mg IV/PO PRN
  • Ensure patent IV prior to injection — extravasation causes serious tissue injury

Diet & Fluids.name: Diet Advancement
Diet & Fluids.summary: Full Liquids Plus on POD 1 (surgical team orders)
Diet & Fluids.content:
Diet Advancement

Advance to Full Liquids Plus on POD 1 — orders by surgical team.

Diet & Fluids.name: IV Fluid Reduction
Diet & Fluids.summary: IVF to 50 cc/hr on POD 1 (surgical team orders)
Diet & Fluids.content:
IV Fluid Reduction

Reduce IVF to 50 cc/hr on POD 1 — orders by surgical team.

protocols.name: Bariatric
protocols.description: Vanderbilt University — Weight Reduction Surgery ERAS Protocol v2.2019

Assessment.name: Pain History Assessment
Assessment.summary: Thorough pain history: opioids, CSMD verification, sites, psych history
Assessment.content:
Pain History Assessment

Orders by Perioperative Consult Service

  • Current opioid and non-opioid pain medications
  • Current opioid provider + CSMD verification
  • Sites of pain and baseline pain scores
  • Previous interventions and efficacy
  • Co-morbid psychiatric conditions and medications

Medications.name: Gabapentin
Medications.summary: 100–300 mg PO 1 hr before OR; higher dose if opioid-dependent (if safe)
Medications.content:
Gabapentin — Preoperative

  • 100–300 mg PO 1 hr before OR
  • Reduce to 100 mg if >65y; consider omitting if >75y
  • Dose reduction for OSA or renal dysfunction
  • Consider higher dose for opioid-dependent patients (if no sedation risk)
  • Patients on home gabapentin: ensure 100–200% of home dose taken AM of surgery

Medications.name: Acetaminophen
Medications.summary: 1000 mg PO night before + 1000 mg PO 1 hr before OR
Medications.content:
Acetaminophen — Preoperative

  • 1000 mg PO night before surgery (managed by bariatric surgery team)
  • 1000 mg PO 1 hr before OR
  • Do not use if Child Class C liver disease

Medications.name: PONV Prophylaxis
Medications.summary: TIVA preferred; no routine scopolamine; agents = # of risk factors
Medications.content:
PONV Prophylaxis — Preoperative

*TIVA preferred for bariatric patients. # of PONV agents = # of risk factors.

  • No routine scopolamine — only for 4 Apfel risk factors with hx of PONV
  • Do NOT use if >65y with OSA/dementia or anticholinergic use

Fluids.name: Carbohydrate Loading
Fluids.summary: Two 12 oz electrolyte + carb drinks morning of surgery (≤2 hrs before OR)
Fluids.content:
Carbohydrate Loading

Managed by Bariatric Surgery Team at pre-op visit

  • Two 12 oz electrolyte + carbohydrate clear liquid drinks morning of surgery
  • Up to 2 hours before surgery
  • Extend to 4 hours prior if advanced diabetes/gastroparesis

Blocks.name: Regional Block — Gastric Sleeve
Blocks.summary: Bilateral RS block + RIGHT oblique subcostal TAP (position 3 or 4)
Blocks.content:
Regional Anesthesia — Gastric Sleeve

Block 1 — Bilateral Rectus Sheath

  • Ropivacaine 0.25% + Dexamethasone 4 mg — 20–30 mL/side

Block 2 — RIGHT Oblique Subcostal TAP (OSTAP)

  • Goal: perform in position 3 or 4
  • Ropivacaine 0.25% + Dexamethasone 4 mg — 30 mL

Total ropivacaine must not exceed 3 mg/kg.

Blocks.name: Regional Block — Gastric Bypass
Blocks.summary: Bilateral rectus sheath blocks only; 20–30 mL/side
Blocks.content:
Regional Anesthesia — Gastric Bypass

  • Bilateral Rectus Sheath Blocks
  • Ropivacaine 0.25% + Dexamethasone 4 mg — 20–30 mL/side

Total ropivacaine must not exceed 3 mg/kg.

Blocks.name: Regional Block — Gastric Bypass (Dr. English)
Blocks.summary: Bilateral RS + LEFT oblique subcostal TAP (position 4); 15–30 mL each
Blocks.content:
Regional Anesthesia — Gastric Bypass (Dr. English)

Block 1 — Bilateral Rectus Sheath

  • Ropivacaine 0.25% + Dexamethasone 4 mg — 15–30 mL/side

Block 2 — LEFT Oblique Subcostal TAP (OSTAP)

  • Goal: position 4
  • Ropivacaine 0.25% + Dexamethasone 4 mg — 15–30 mL

Total ropivacaine must not exceed 3 mg/kg.

Blocks.name: Regional Block — Full Laparotomy (Rare)
Blocks.summary: Thoracic epidural placement by Perioperative Service
Blocks.content:
Regional Anesthesia — Full Laparotomy (Rare)

Thoracic Epidural Placement by Perioperative Service.

Anesthesia.name: TIVA with Propofol + BIS
Anesthesia.summary: 25–75 mcg/kg/min adjunct or 200 mcg/kg/min full TIVA; BIS monitor required without volatile
Anesthesia.content:
TIVA with Propofol

  • Adjunct to volatile: 25–75 mcg/kg/min
  • Full TIVA: 200 mcg/kg/min
  • Use BIS monitor without volatile agent
  • If on ≥1.3 MAC volatile: contact attending

Anesthesia.name: Opioid Minimization
Anesthesia.summary: No induction opioids; ketamine + lidocaine; esmolol for HR control
Anesthesia.content:
Opioid Minimization

  • No induction opioids
  • Propofol + ketamine + lidocaine
  • Esmolol for heart rate control

Anesthesia.name: Ketamine
Anesthesia.summary: 0.5 mg/kg → 5 mcg/kg/min until fascia closed → 2.5 mcg/kg/min until emergence
Anesthesia.content:
Ketamine — Intraoperative

Use low concentration bag (100 mg/100 cc).

  • 0.5 mg/kg IV bolus at induction
  • 5 mcg/kg/min until fascia closed
  • 2.5 mcg/kg/min until emergence
  • Reduce bolus to 0.25 mg/kg if >65y

Anesthesia.name: Lidocaine Infusion
Anesthesia.summary: 1.5 mg/kg bolus then 2 mg/min to case end; restart in PACU
Anesthesia.content:
Lidocaine Infusion

  • 1.5 mg/kg bolus + 2 mg/min drip to case end
  • Restart in PACU per postop order set

Anesthesia.name: Methadone
Anesthesia.summary: Opioid exposed (1–60 MED) or tolerant (>60 MED): 5–10 mg IV after induction, max 20 mg
Anesthesia.content:
Methadone

Most patients do not require intraoperative opioids.

  • Opioid exposed (1–60 MED) or tolerant (>60 MED): 5–10 mg IV after induction, max 20 mg
  • Otherwise: assess at emergence; 5 mg IV boluses q5–10 min PRN

Anesthesia.name: PONV Prophylaxis — Intraoperative
Anesthesia.summary: Dexamethasone 8 mg; ondansetron 4 mg 30 min pre-emergence; haloperidol 1 mg at skin closure
Anesthesia.content:
PONV Prophylaxis — Intraoperative

  • Dexamethasone: 8 mg IV after induction (unless in TAP blocks)
  • Ondansetron: 4 mg IV 30 min before emergence
  • Haloperidol: 1 mg IV at skin closure (0.5 mg if >65y) — do not give with known QTc prolongation — current vial 5 mg/mL: BE CAREFUL

Fluid & Hemodynamic Management.name: Intraoperative Fluid Strategy
Fluid & Hemodynamic Management.summary: LR/Plasma-Lyte only; 2–3 mL/kg/hr; norepinephrine preferred; median ~1600 mL
Fluid & Hemodynamic Management.content:
Fluid Strategy

  • LR or Plasma-Lyte/Normosol only — NO NORMAL SALINE
  • Maintenance: 2–3 mL/kg/hr + boluses per clinical course
  • Norepinephrine preferred vasopressor (2–10 mcg/min via peripheral IV)
  • Median case IVF: ~1600 mL
  • Target UOP ≥0.5 mL/kg/hr if Foley present

Pain Management.name: NSAIDs — Avoid
Pain Management.summary: Should be avoided; only consider if all multimodal has failed and opioid sedation present
Pain Management.content:
NSAIDs — Bariatric Patients

NSAIDs should be avoided. Consider only after all multimodal options have failed and patient has opioid-related sedation. Surgical attending discussion required.

Pain Management.name: Lidocaine Infusion
Pain Management.summary: 24 hrs postop: <70 kg→1 mg/min, 70–100→1.5 (~20 cc/hr), >100→2 mg/min (~30 cc/hr)
Pain Management.content:
Lidocaine Infusion — Postoperative

24 hours postop (can extend based on pain/PO intake).

WeightDoseRate
<70 kg1 mg/min IV~15 cc/hr
70–100 kg1.5 mg/min IV~20 cc/hr
>100 kg2 mg/min IV~30 cc/hr

Pain Management.name: Gabapentin
Pain Management.summary: 300–600 mg PO q8h POD0; renally adjusted; continue ×14 days at discharge
Pain Management.content:
Gabapentin — Postoperative

  • 300–600 mg PO q8h POD0 to discharge
  • Lower dose for >65y or sedation/dizziness
CrClDose
>60 mL/min300–1200 mg TID
30–59 mL/min200–700 mg BID
15–29 mL/min200–700 mg daily
<15 mL/min100–300 mg daily
ESRD/HDPer CrCl + 125–250 mg after dialysis

Pain Management.name: Acetaminophen
Pain Management.summary: 1000 mg PO q8h POD0 to discharge; 500 mg q8h for liver disease
Pain Management.content:
Acetaminophen — Postoperative

  • 1000 mg PO q8h POD0 to discharge (max 4000 mg/day)
  • 500 mg q8h for liver disease; avoid if Child Class C

Pain Management.name: Opioid PRN
Pain Management.summary: Tramadol first line; oxycodone second; hydromorphone IV for breakthrough only
Pain Management.content:
Opioid PRN

Opioid-Naive

  • Tramadol 50–100 mg q4–6h PRN (caution with SSRI/SNRI/TCAs)
  • Oxycodone 5 mg PO q4h PRN >5/10
  • Hydromorphone IV PRN only if not controlled by other modalities

Chronic Opioid Therapy

Ensure meeting 100–125% of home oral dose. Perioperative Service should be involved.

Pain Management.name: Rescue Plan — Suboptimal Analgesia
Pain Management.summary: 4-step rescue: H&P → severity → pain type → rescue treatment
Pain Management.content:
Rescue Plan for Suboptimal Analgesia

StepActionDetails
1Focused H&PPreop analgesia, baseline pain, postop exam — location & etiology
2Assess SeverityLocation, severity, duration, aggravating factors; functional limitations; adverse drug events
3Pain TypeNeuropathic, inflammatory, visceral, or somatic? Exclude surgical complications first.
4Rescue TreatmentConfirm all non-opioid options used. Add opioid PO if tolerated, IV if needed.

PONV Treatment.name: PONV Ladder
PONV Treatment.content:
PONV Treatment Ladder

LineAgentDose
1stOndansetron4 mg IV/PO q6h PRN
2ndHaloperidol0.5–1 mg IV q4–6h PRN
3rdScopolamine patchOnly if active PONV despite above
4thPromethazine6.25–12.5 mg IV/PO q4h PRN (ensure patent IV)

Re-consult Periop Service if uncontrolled. Alternatives: dronabinol (avoid >65y), dexamethasone (discuss with surgery), aprepitant (pharmacy approval needed).

Post-Discharge.name: Discharge Opioid Plan
Post-Discharge.summary: Taper 30–50% by day 3 post-discharge; if no opioid use 24 hrs pre-discharge → 3-pill rescue only
Post-Discharge.content:
Discharge Opioid Plan

Follow TN Together Opioid Prescribing Guidelines. Prescribe the opioid used in hospital.

  • Tramadol 50–100 mg q6h PRN (Schedule IV, refillable by phone; no SSRI/SNRI/TCAs)
  • Oxycodone 5 mg q6h PRN (second line)
  • Taper ~30–50% by 3 days post-discharge, then off
  • If no opioid use 24 hrs before discharge → 3-pill rescue pack only

Post-Discharge.name: Discharge Multimodal Medications
Post-Discharge.summary: Acetaminophen max 3 g/day ×3 days; gabapentin ×14 days; muscle relaxants/alpha-2 ×7 days PRN
Post-Discharge.content:
Discharge Multimodal Medications

  • Acetaminophen: max 3 g/day × 3 days
  • Gabapentin: × 14 days (taper if on >300 mg q8h)
  • Muscle relaxants: PRN × 7 days
  • Alpha-2 agonists: PRN × 7 days or until off opioids

Discharge Nausea

  • Ondansetron 4 mg ODT q6h PRN
  • Promethazine PO 12.5 mg q4h PRN
  • Scopolamine patch if persisting
